title: GameStop sales jump on collectibles, hardware growth
text: GameStop achieved strong Q2 2025 results with net sales increasing 21.8% year-over-year to $972.2 million. The company reported net income of $168.6 million, a dramatic jump from $14.8 million in the prior year period. The retailer's sales expansion was primarily fueled by growth in the collectibles category, with the company also benefiting from increased hardware and accessories sales. This represents a turnaround from Q1 performance, which showed sharp double-digit declines in software, hardware and accessories. Beyond traditional retail, GameStop has diversified its revenue streams by purchasing 4,710 Bitcoin during Q2 valued around $500 million at purchase, with holdings worth $528.6 million by quarter-end. The company divested Canadian operations and is closing French locations to streamline international presence. GameStop closed approximately 600 U.S. locations in fiscal 2024, with plans to close a significant number of additional stores in fiscal 2025. CEO Ryan Cohen reflected on results with the statement not bad for a piece of crap retailer, signaling confidence despite historical retail industry challenges.
